description: Native Nigerian English (Yoruba, Lagos), under five decades of flat American Detroit vowel. Male, early 60s. High quality recording. Persona: wry retired foundryman. Emotion: warm, dry-witted, watchful. Timbre is a low once-deep-chested baritone now thinned and a little short of breath, the Yoruba music nearly worn off but surfacing on certain words. Pacing is unhurried and lightly percussive, short observations offered like small gifts, going quieter and courtlier under strain rather than louder.
text: [warm] I know your feet... heard you before you said a word. [serious] So don't tell me it's fine. My mother's doctor said fine, right up until it wasn't. Sit. Tell me true, and I'll forgive the breakfast.
